**Letovice** ([German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Lettowitz*) is a town in [Blansko District](/wiki/Blansko_District "Blansko District") in the [South Moravian Region](/wiki/South_Moravian_Region "South Moravian Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 7,100 inhabitants.

## Administrative division

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Administrative division")]

Letovice consists of 17 municipal parts (in brackets population according to the 2021 census):[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* Letovice (4,210)
* Babolky (50)
* Chlum (49)
* Dolní Smržov (112)
* Jasinov (96)
* Kladoruby (214)
* Klevetov (29)
* Kněževísko (35)
* Kochov (92)
* Lhota (55)
* Meziříčko (190)
* Novičí (82)
* Podolí (83)
* Slatinka (71)
* Třebětín (1,008)
* Zábludov (102)
* Zboněk (126)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Letovice is located about 21 kilometres (13 mi) north of [Blansko](/wiki/Blansko "Blansko") and 37 km (23 mi) north of [Brno](/wiki/Brno "Brno"). It lies mostly in the [Boskovice Furrow](/w/index.php?title=Boskovice_Furrow&action=edit&redlink=1 "Boskovice Furrow (page does not exist)"). The highest point is the hill Ve Vrších at 590 m (1,940 ft) above sea level. The town is situated in the valley of the [Svitava](/wiki/Svitava_(river) "Svitava (river)") River, at its confluence with the [Křetínka](/wiki/K%C5%99et%C3%ADnka "Křetínka") River. West of the town is the [Letovice Reservoir](/wiki/K%C5%99et%C3%ADnka#Bodies_of_water "Křetínka"), built on the Křetínka.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

The first written mention of Letovice is from 1145.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Transport")]

The I/43 road (part of the [European route E461](/wiki/European_route_E461 "European route E461")) from Brno to [Svitavy](/wiki/Svitavy "Svitavy") passes through the market town.

Letovice is located on the railway line [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague")–Brno.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Sights")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/Letovice%2C_z%C3%A1mek_%282519%29.jpg/250px-Letovice%2C_z%C3%A1mek_%282519%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Letovice,_z%C3%A1mek_(2519).jpg)

Letovice Castle

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Kl%C3%A1%C5%A1ter_milosrdn%C3%BDch_brat%C5%99%C3%AD_%28Letovice%29.JPG/250px-Kl%C3%A1%C5%A1ter_milosrdn%C3%BDch_brat%C5%99%C3%AD_%28Letovice%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:Kl%C3%A1%C5%A1ter_milosrdn%C3%BDch_brat%C5%99%C3%AD_(Letovice).JPG)

Former monastery

Among the main landmarks of the town is the Letovice Castle. The original castle from the 13th century was rebuilt into an early Baroque residence at the end of the 17th century. Today the castle is privately owned and gradually repaired with the aim of making it available to the public.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The Church of Saint Procopius was built in the Gothic style in the last quarter of the 14th century. It is an important historical and architectural monument. A covered staircase connects the church with the town centre.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The former monastery of the [Merciful Brothers](/wiki/Brothers_Hospitallers_of_Saint_John_of_God "Brothers Hospitallers of Saint John of God") was founded in 1747. The monastery complex with the Church of Saint George and a hospital was built in the Baroque style in 1751–1784. Part of the monastery is a unique restored Baroque pharmacy from 1784, open to the public. Today, the entire premises of the former monastery belong to the Letovice Hospital.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Count Gustav Kálnoky](/wiki/Count_Gustav_K%C3%A1lnoky "Count Gustav Kálnoky") (1832–1898), Austro-Hungarian diplomat and statesman
* [Alois Biach](/wiki/Alois_Biach "Alois Biach") (1849–1918), Austrian physician and medical writer
* [Emanuel Löffler](/wiki/Emanuel_L%C3%B6ffler "Emanuel Löffler") (1901–1986), gymnast

## Twin towns – sister cities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Letovice&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Twin towns – sister cities")]

See also: [List of twin towns and sister cities in the Czech Republic](/wiki/List_of_twin_towns_and_sister_cities_in_the_Czech_Republic "List of twin towns and sister cities in the Czech Republic")

Letovice is [twinned](/wiki/Sister_city "Sister city") with:[[11]](#cite_note-11)

* [![Poland](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/1/12/Flag_of_Poland.svg/40px-Flag_of_Poland.svg.png)](/wiki/Poland "Poland") [Chełmno](/wiki/Che%C5%82mno "Chełmno"), Poland
* [![Germany](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/b/ba/Flag_of_Germany.svg/40px-Flag_of_Germany.svg.png)](/wiki/Germany "Germany") [Kirchlinteln](/wiki/Kirchlinteln "Kirchlinteln"), Germany
* [![Hungary](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Flag_of_Hungary.svg/40px-Flag_of_Hungary.svg.png)](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") [Kőbánya (Budapest)](/wiki/K%C5%91b%C3%A1nya "Kőbánya"), Hungary
* [![Slovakia](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e6/Flag_of_Slovakia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Slovakia.svg.png)](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia") [Slepčany](/wiki/Slep%C4%8Dany "Slepčany"), Slovakia
* [![Croatia](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Flag_of_Croatia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Croatia.svg.png)](/wiki/Croatia "Croatia") [Stari Grad](/wiki/Stari_Grad,_Croatia "Stari Grad, Croatia"), Croatia
